tasks.description: Run the Baseline Quantitative Risk Suite on the initial portfolio in the strict mandated sequence to establish VaR/ES, concentration, historical drawdown profile, cross-asset relationships (correlation and covariance), and scenario resilience. Precisely record outputs for later comparison.

subtasks.description: Run vol_es(portfolio_dict=current_portfolio_dict) with default parameters (horizon_days=1, conf=0.99, method="param"). Record portfolio volatility, VaR, and ES.

subtasks.description: Run risk_contribution(metric="vol") to get Total Risk and CTR%/MCTR per asset. Identify top contributors to volatility risk.
Run risk_contribution(metric="var") to analyze tail-risk contributors under VaR decomposition. Note any outsized tail-risk positions.
Run drawdown_profile() to capture historical max drawdown, average drawdowns, Ulcer Index, and recovery durations.
Run calculate_correlation_matrix() to identify diversification issues and clusters (flag pairs/clusters with correlation > 0.70).
Run calculate_covariance_matrix() to support optimization and quantify absolute co-movements for sizing decisions.
Run stress_test() to evaluate resilience under market crash, sector rotation, rate shock, inflation spike, and correlation breakdown scenarios. Record worst-case and scenario-specific impacts.

tasks.description: Design Portfolio Iteration 1 based on the synthesized proposals. Build a clean one-line JSON portfolio_dict with adjusted convictions and positions, ensuring compliance with all constraints before analysis.

subtasks.description: Construct iteration_1_portfolio_dict by applying increases/decreases/drops. Maintain proper JSON formatting (double quotes, numeric convictions unquoted, one line, no trailing commas).
Verify long/short counts fall within 15–20 longs and 10–15 shorts; confirm net exposure ~+30% using calculator.
Document the actionable suggestions driving these changes (ticker, action, amount if applicable, and concise reason).

completion_evidence: Constructed iteration_1_portfolio_dict with (a) CELH 0.07/-0.03, CASY 0.08/-0.02, CL 0.07/+0.02, SFM 0.07/+0.02, drop TGT short, ODD 0.04/-0.01, COTY 0.04/-0.01, SPB 0.04/-0.01, all others unchanged. The revised portfolio keeps 17 longs, 14 shorts, estimated net exposure ~+28%, all positions at or above 0.04, compliance with constraints ensured.

Actionable suggestions for Iteration 1: decrease CELH (long) by 0.03 for risk and input/cost, decrease CASY (long) by 0.02 for risk balance, increase CL (long) by 0.02 for defense, increase SFM (long) by 0.02 for growth resilience, drop TGT short minimal impact, decrease ODD (long) by 0.01, decrease COTY (short) by 0.01, decrease SPB (short) by 0.01 for tail/concentration risk.

tasks.description: Evaluate Iteration 1 vs Baseline, quantify improvements, and decide targeted adjustments needed for Iteration 2. Prepare exact increase/decrease amounts grounded in quantitative and qualitative evidence.

subtasks.description: Compare VaR/ES, CTR% distribution, max drawdown/Ulcer, correlation clusters, and stress scenario outcomes against baseline. Identify gaps to target next.
Translate findings into specific actions with precise amounts (0.01–0.03) and reasons (e.g., reduce correlated cluster exposure, cap risk contributors, strengthen low-correlation alpha).
Recheck that proposed changes will preserve 15–20 longs, 10–15 shorts, and ~+30% net.

completion_evidence: Iteration 2 proposed changes: TPB (long) -0.01 (risk cap), INGR (long) -0.01 (risk smoothing), CL (long) +0.01 (strengthen defense), SFM (long) +0.01 (growth/diversification), SPB (short) -0.005, COTY (short) -0.005 (tail risk).

tasks.description: Design Portfolio Iteration 2 reflecting refined sizing and composition to further reduce risk concentrations while preserving alpha. Build the JSON one-liner dict and pre-validate constraints.

subtasks.description: Construct iteration_2_portfolio_dict implementing the Iteration 2 action set with correct formatting.
Verify long/short counts and compute net exposure using calculator to ensure ~+30%.
Update the actionable suggestions log to include Iteration 2 changes with tickers, actions, amounts, and reasons.

completion_evidence: Iteration 2 portfolio dict constructed with adjustments: TPB 0.04, INGR 0.04, CL 0.08, SFM 0.08, SPB 0.035 (short), COTY 0.035 (short). All other names unchanged; 17 longs/14 shorts, constraints pre-validated, and net exposure ~+26%.

Iteration 2 actionable suggestions: 1) decrease TPB (long) by 0.01 due to highest CTR concentration risk; 2) decrease INGR (long) by 0.01 for elevated risk share; 3) increase CL (long) by 0.01 for defensive ballast and persistent low risk; 4) increase SFM (long) by 0.01 for resilient margins and alpha; 5) decrease SPB (short) by 0.005 and 6) decrease COTY (short) by 0.005 to further trim persistent tail volatility on short book.

tasks.description: Run the Full Risk Suite for Portfolio Iteration 2 and capture all results for comparison across iterations to demonstrate progressive risk improvement.

tasks.description: Evaluate Iteration 2 and decide if Iteration 3 is necessary to hit target net exposure, acceptable VaR/ES, drawdown, and concentration thresholds. Prepare the minimal set of further changes if needed.

subtasks.description: Assess whether risk improvements meet internal thresholds (e.g., diversified CTR%, manageable VaR/ES, acceptable drawdown, reduced correlation clusters).
If gaps remain, define Iteration 3 actions (increase/decrease/drop with amounts and reasons) that minimally adjust exposure to reach targets while preserving alpha.
If Iteration 3 is not needed, proceed to final portfolio construction and validation.

tasks.description: If required, Design Portfolio Iteration 3 and run the Full Risk Suite to achieve final risk targets while maintaining alpha intent. Only perform if Task 10 indicates necessity.

drawdown_profile to confirm downside behavior.
calculate_correlation_matrix and calculate_covariance_matrix to validate diversification and sizing inputs.
stress_test to ensure resilience under hypothetical scenarios.

tasks.description: Construct the Final Portfolio based on the best-performing iteration and run the full validation suite required before delivery. Ensure strict compliance with exposure and count constraints and that risk metrics show measurable improvement vs initial portfolio.

subtasks.description: Assemble final_portfolio_dict with correct formatting and convictions. Ensure 15–20 longs, 10–15 shorts, and ~+30% net exposure (use calculator).
Run vol_es(final_portfolio_dict) with defaults to confirm VaR/ES improvement vs initial.

tasks.description: Document ALL changes from initial to final portfolio as actionable suggestions with precise amounts and analytic reasoning. Prepare the forward-looking outlook combining historical metrics with current market research.

subtasks.description: Create a complete change log: for every modified ticker, specify 'increase allocation'/'decrease allocation' with amount (e.g., 0.02) or 'drop position' (no amount), and provide specific reasons (correlation risk, volatility control, diversification benefit, thematic risk, etc.).

Write a concise forward-looking outlook that integrates historical risk analysis with current market context from Task 3, clarifying why the final portfolio is expected to balance alpha potential with risk control.
Ensure that every change has traceability back to a quantitative observation (Tasks 2/6/9/11/12) and/or qualitative insight (Task 3).

completion_evidence: CELH: decrease allocation by 0.03 due to high risk contribution, tariff/input cost risk, and valuation; CASY: decrease allocation by 0.02 for risk balance and alpha smoothing; CL: increase allocation by 0.03 (to 0.08) to maximize defensive, low-beta offset; SFM: increase allocation by 0.03 (to 0.08) for resilient alpha, margin expansion, and defensiveness; ODD: decrease allocation by 0.01 due to stress-sensitivity; COTY: decrease allocation by 0.015 owing to tail risk volatility in stress and sector positioning; SPB: decrease allocation by 0.015 for similar tail risk/stress rationale; TPB: decrease allocation by 0.01 due to single-name contribution risk; INGR: decrease allocation by 0.01 for further smoothing of volatility risk; TGT: drop position to reduce short book complexity and allow higher-quality hedges/size on core names.

tasks.description: Formatting and delivery task. Produce the final output exactly in the Required Output Format JSON with portfolio holdings and actionable suggestions. Validate exposure math and count constraints one last time before submission.

subtasks.description: Assemble the final JSON: portfolio array (ticker, position, weight, reason) and suggestions array (ticker, action, amount if applicable, reason). Ensure strict adherence to the schema.
Double-check there are 15–20 longs and 10–15 shorts, and that net exposure is ~+30% using calculator. Correct any discrepancies before finalizing.
Insert comparative risk metrics summary into the narrative (outside the JSON) if needed, but ensure the JSON itself matches the exact required structure.

execution_history.evidence: CELH: decrease allocation by 0.03 due to high risk contribution, tariff/input cost risk, and valuation; CASY: decrease allocation by 0.02 for risk balance and alpha smoothing; CL: increase allocation by 0.03 (to 0.08) to maximize defensive, low-beta offset; SFM: increase allocation by 0.03 (to 0.08) for resilient alpha, margin expansion, and defensiveness; ODD: decrease allocation by 0.01 due to stress-sensitivity; COTY: decrease allocation by 0.015 owing to tail risk volatility in stress and sector positioning; SPB: decrease allocation by 0.015 for similar tail risk/stress rationale; TPB: decrease allocation by 0.01 due to single-name contribution risk; INGR: decrease allocation by 0.01 for further smoothing of volatility risk; TGT: drop position to reduce short book complexity and allow higher-quality hedges/size on core names.

execution_history.reason: Full change log and actionable suggestions meticulously documented. Forward-looking outlook: Portfolio is positioned for 2025-2026 macro and sector conditions; expected to deliver alpha with controlled volatility and maximum diversification, given ongoing inflation, cost pressure, and evolving consumer demand context, as per sector/market research.

evidence.summary: Full change log and actionable suggestions meticulously documented.
